Video.summary: Daimlers and Morgans, and Citroens, oh my! Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation grows stronger as Bob Boston’s collection, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattles into an event; guest host Katherine Legge explains the competitor to her racing sponsor Acura, Porsche.
Video.title: Boston's (Un)Common Collection

Video.summary: Early spring in Jekyll Island, GA is something to behold. Add a few hundred of the country’s coolest rides for Caffeine & Octane at the Beach, and it’s a thing of beauty. Models that blasted the glass ceiling of expectation at the all makes and models show include a hand-built, twin-turbo Tucker, a one-off 1951 Kaiser Drag’N, and a Roadkill Custom known only as Blasphemi!
Video.title: C&O at the Beach

Video.summary: Imagination, ingenuity, and heartache join friends who set out with a simple goal of transforming a ‘35 Plymouth into a running taxi cab. Provenance and passion take center stage as families and friends strive for best in show. Host Rickey Gadson checks out perfect replicas of the original Shelby Cobras and takes Skip and Bryan on ‘borrowed’ taxi ride.
Video.title: Big Yellow Taxi

Video.summary: Call the Peach State Challengers a “car club” and you will quickly be corrected. The defenders of everything Dodge are “family” and they make no excuses for their loyalty to brand and brother. Spend a day at the track with these Challengers and find out what their version of the family photo looks like.
Video.title: Peach State Challengers

Video.summary: Former Jet Ski Champion Ryan Hardwick has succeeded in everything he’s touched in racing and business, so why not race Lamborghinis! Hardwick has found rapid success with his high tech training methods; he shares his secrets with host Rickey Gadson in this episode of C&O.
Video.title: Lambo Racer

Video.summary: It’s crunch time at Graveyard Carz, Mark and his cousin Dougie fire up the engine for our Turbine Bronze Charger while Dave assembles its firewall in preparation to install the drivetrain.
Video.title: Frankenstein's Work

Video.summary: Dave is ready to wrap up the 1970 Challenger R/T SE but is waiting for parts. Meanwhile, Mark’s A100 build continues, and after a long and tedious body-work and paint phase, Will is ready to deliver Dave the 1969 B5 Blue Plymouth GTX.
Video.title: Look to the West for B5 Blue

Image.alt: Look to the West for B5 Blue
Look to the West for B5 Blue
Look to the West for B5 Blue

Video.summary: The Ghoulz have finished the 1970 Challenger R/T SE. As they take it out for the first test drive in over 15 years, Mark and the team reflect on the trials and challenges throughout the restoration process.
Video.title: A Challenger Reflected

Video.summary: Mark, Dougie, and Allysa team up to kick things into high-gear around the shop. They begin the restoration of a Challenger T/A, Will paints a 1968 GTX in B7 Blue, Allysa learns how dashes are assembled, and they install the drivetrain for the ultra rare, 1 of 2, 1970 Hemi Convertible Coronet R/T.
Video.title: Mark, Dougie, Allysa, Oh My!

Video.summary: Mark pulls back the curtain on the next four Mopar’s in desperate need of resurrection while also showcasing the fans favorite moments from Seasons 8 and 9.
Video.title: Shaking the Magic 8-Ball

Video.summary: Mark and the Ghouls are back, and they are ready to get to work! With a call in from the owner of the super rare 1 of 2 ever made, 1970 Coronet Hemi Convertible, Mark has to work fast to reach a looming deadline. At the same time, Will primes the body of our 1969 Daytona, bringing it one step closer to being back on the road.
Video.title: Rapping Up the Dash

Video.summary: While Mark is on a road test of the just completed 1968 Charger, its owner surprises the shop by arriving in person! While the owner waits, Will completes the paint on a beautiful 1970 Challenger in B5 blue. Finally, with Mark returned the owner receives his restored Charger and cooks a meal for the Ghouls as thanks!
Video.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

Video.summary: It’s time to hit the casino! Mark has been invited to a massive car show at the Little Creek Casino but before he can go there are a few things that need to be finished first. Mark and Doug disassemble the Phoenix ‘Cuda, a car that survived a massive garage fire. At the car show, Allysa and Will interact with fans and find their “pick of the show.”
Video.title: Upshift Creek

Video.summary: Mark has once again taken Allysa under his wing and is teaching her the finer points of installing a grill and headlights on our 1972 Duster, meanwhile George is in the body shop attaching a tricky quarter panel to a 1969 ‘Cuda which Mark has to check off the list…military style? Finally, the Duster gets it’s drive train and is finally reunited with its engine.
Video.title: It's A Duster Buster

Video.summary: Nick Rutter walks you through step-by-step on how to remove stubborn bug splatter from your vehicle along with tips and techniques for using a waterless wash as well as a clay bar.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 1

Video.summary: Nick Rutter provides tips and techniques for restoring dull, faded paint using a forced-rotation dual action polisher along with a graphene 3-in-1 ceramic polish, along with severe scratch and scuff removal.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 2

Video.summary: State-of-the-art engineering and passion projects take center stage on this edition of Caffeine & Octane. The revolutionary Delta Wing Racer turns on hot lap after hot lap at Road Atlanta, and driver Katherine Legge offers insight on how “less is more” when it comes to power to weight ratios; the dark world of a graffiti artist paints a picture of a car called Vengeance while the hosts share the eclectic hybrids of machines that make it to Caffeine and Octane with Vengance’s creator Totem.
Video.title: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Image.alt: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Video.summary: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the ultimate scene and the place to be seen. Even after 12 hours of racing at the Petit Le Mans endurance race, Henry Ford III, the great grandson of Henry Ford the industrial magnate and current member of Ford’s Board of Directors, showed up to take in the sights, address the crowds, and sign a one of a kind Ford Shelby GT350R. The GT350R will hit the road with Operation Hope to encourage young people to study math and science through motorsports; Covert car counterfeiter, Chuck Beck looks for Ford’s approval on his own version of a muscled up Festiva. YouTuber ‘That Dude in Blue’ David Patterson joins Skip and Bryan to get a special look at the motor-based philanthropic outreach and amazing cars that come with it done by John Hope Bryant, friend of Caffeine and Octane.
Video.title: Henry Ford III & Counterfeit Cars

Video.summary: Rickey and Bryan race outside of their comfort zones, each competing to find the raddest car collection. Rickey hangs out with classic racer, Ron Green, who shows him the ropes in the ultra light '83 Ralt RT5. Bryan visits a collection of 120 racing motorcycle owned by mechanic and racer Mike McConville. Mike takes Bryan motorcross racing in his backyard after showing off his high horsepowered car collection. Caffeine and Octane alongside Ron Green visit Atlanta's first Concours d'elegance to look at classic cars that have been perfectly maintained for decades.
Video.title: Go Fast Collections

Video.summary: Wayne Phears’ Jaguar E-Type was nothing more than a diamond in the rough before the mineral rights attorney took on the basket case. Caffeine & Octane brings viewers the story of how Phears’, through relentless research and determination, restored and reunited the #10 Jag that Enzo Ferrari called “the most beautiful car ever made”; Atlanta Wraps shows how wrapping Corvettes and Lamborghinis can color a car fast. Caffeine and Exotics is featured again to show off the coolest wraps Atlanta can supply.
Video.title: The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars

Image.alt: The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars
The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars
The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars

Video.summary: Twenty-five years of hand building record-setting race cars is no easy task. But don’t tell that to Don Panoz who takes on Porsches and Ferraris with his laid back personality and a full-production facility that makes improvised versions of Americas V-8’s; Caffeine & Octane gets an exclusive look at an elusive man’s life long pursuit of everything Chevrolet.
Video.title: Panoz Racing & Chasing Chevys

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane goes old school with Psycho Deville singer and pin striper Walt Richards. Richards has less than a week to hand paint his signature flames on a way cool ’50 Mercury. An introduction to Retrofit Lighting’s trickle down technology - from cars to motorcycles - that will revolutionize how bikes will see and be seen.
Video.title: Retrofitting a Psycho Mercury

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation is tested when over 100 VW’s, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattled into an event. The annual VW Invasion has swarmed C&O, bringing with it Bugs, Beetles, Buses and more by a very dedicated VW fan base.
Video.title: Beetles, Bugs, and Buses, Oh My!

Image.alt: Beetles, Bugs, and Buses, Oh My!
Beetles, Bugs, and Buses, Oh My!
Beetles, Bugs, and Buses, Oh My!

Video.summary: Rapper, radio, and ATL TV personality Darian “Big Tigger” Morgan invites Caffeine & Octane into his home. Cameras look past his collection of 500 Michael Jordan shoes to focus their lenses on a one-off motorcycle the 6–time NBA Champion gave to Tigger as a gift; A second to none, muscle car collection by one of the founders of Monster Energy gets toured by one of our hosts.
Video.title: Big Tigger v. Monster Muscle

Video.summary: Vintage Testarossas and Quatra Valve Ferraris don’t just show up on a dealer’s floor, they’re hunted! Adam Merlin, exotic car expert, has spent a lifetime searching for the cars that defined his youth, but nothing is assumed or given in his high stakes game of Risk; A muscle car fiend shows off his private collection to host Skip Smith.
Video.title: Exotic Car Hunter

Video.summary: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the major league of North American car shows. Former Atlanta Brave, World Series star, and 10-Time Gold Glove winner Andruw Jones acknowledged the stardom and showed up to show off his 6 wheeled Super Jeep. The state of the art creation took years in planning and execution, but Jones chooses one great event for its debut.
Video.title: Andruw Jones' Super Jeep

Video.summary: The natural landscape of Jekyll Island, GA makes for a great backdrop to showcase the country’s greatest cars. But the ocean air and remoteness of this GA barrier island is no place to leave a painstakingly rebuilt ‘59 Imperial Speedster. Host Skip Smith makes it his mission to locate the talented builder behind this island mystery. Guest host Totem joins Bryan Fuller in the C&O lots to pick out his favorites.
Video.title: Jekyll and Hide

Video.summary: Racing Legend Don Garlitts drops by the garage to get some help doing some upgrades to his Chrysler 300.
Video.title: I Don’t Drive What I Can’t Hear

Image.alt: I Don’t Drive What I Can’t Hear
I Don’t Drive What I Can’t Hear
I Don’t Drive What I Can’t Hear

Video.summary: Willie and Kevin go through the steps it takes to make sure a car you are sending off with your child to college is reliable for years to come.
Video.title: Time for a Change

Video.summary: Willie and Kevin pull out all of the power on a modified C6 vette.
Video.title: Twin Turbo Tuning

Video.summary: Willie brings a Mopar out of the stable to get a much needed suspension upgrade and the tranny.
Video.title: Willie’s Road Runner

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie B. put some personal touches on a Wildcat UTV.
Video.title: Artic Cat

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie get rid of the tired old leafspring and replace it with a monster torque arm suspension
Video.title: 69 Camaro Suspension Upgrade

Video.summary: The guys work on a new Challenger while reminiscing about the Mopars of old.
Video.title: Mopar Love

Video.summary: Willie works out the dent on a new custom hood.
Video.title: Custom Repair

Video.summary: Who better to explain the history and discuss the changes, year to year, of the Dodge Charger, than the Master of Mopar himself, Mark Worman. In this special episode of Graveyard Carz, Mark will show you unique differences, surprising insights, and cool features on multiple years of the same car: the Dodge Charger.
Video.title: Carbituary: Charger

Video.summary: The focus has shifted, with parts having arrived at the shop it’s time to install the Grill, Bumper, Dash and Interior of our 1968 Turbine Bronze Charger. Mark and the Ghouls work their magic on the Charger until a familiar face shows up. With Royal there, Mark shifts the crew back to the 1 of 2 1970 Coronet and finishes the interior. Then, it’s time for a test drive!
Video.title: 1.5 Million Dollar Baby
